subject: Databases
title: Batch Processing
description: Learn Batch Processing in one minute.

hook.text: Inserting 100,000 rows one at a time: 90 seconds. Batched in groups of 1,000: 2 seconds.

buildup.text: Each individual INSERT has overhead: parse, plan, execute, commit. Batching amortizes that overhead.

discovery.text: Use multi-row INSERT: INSERT INTO users VALUES (...), (...), (...). One statement, many rows.

twist.text: COPY (Postgres) and LOAD DATA (MySQL) are even faster for bulk loads. They bypass the SQL parser.

climax.text: Wrap batches in a single transaction. One commit instead of 100,000 commits saves enormous overhead.

punchline.text: Batch writes. Reduce round trips. Commit once. Done.

quiz.question: Why is the COPY command faster than multi-row INSERT for bulk loads?
options: It bypasses the SQL parser and writes data directly in the database's internal format
It doesn't require any disk writes
